Anunoby 25' 3PT Step Back Jump Shot (9 PTS) (Hart 2 AST)

Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 16 points with 6 lead changes and 51 ties. The game saw 10 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 59%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q3, 9:49, Hart (NYK): Hart 1' Running Dunk (14 PTS) (Brunson 6 AST). It swung the win probability by +15.2 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q2, 0:30.9, Hart (NYK): Hart 25' 3PT Jump Shot (12 PTS) (Brunson 5 AST). It swung the win probability by -15.0 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 12, away team 8.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q3, 10:09: Brunson 28' 3PT Pullup Jump Shot (5 PTS) (Hart 3 AST). That single play moved NYK's chance of winning from 54% to 72%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went NYK's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Hart of NYK. Across 40 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 64 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 4:26, with NYK down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 37%. In other words: possible, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q2, 0:30.9, with the game tilting NYK's way. The play in the middle of it: Hart 25' 3PT Jump Shot (12 PTS) (Brunson 5 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
